Items.Title: Python概念学习点1：方法和函数的区别 | Python Concept learning point 1: Difference between methods and functions
Items.Description:
哈马修·陈

发表于由于我总是对方法和功能感到困惑，因此我对它们进行了一些阅读，这就是我所理解的。

什么让我困惑

  • 方法和函数看起来一样
  • 他们都有括号（）
  • 他们都可以争论
  • 定义时，名称前都有def

方法和功能的区别

  1. 方法不能自己调用​​，它需要一个对象来调用，函数可以自己调用。
  2. 方法需要和点运算符，函数不需要
  3. 方法必须在类中定义，函数可以自己定义
  4. 定义时，方法至少需要一个参数，第一个必须是self ，函数可以有任意数量的参数（包括0）

以下两个程序将输出“Bloom”，但有什么区别？

示例 1：方法

class Car : def make_sound ( self ): print "Bloom!" ferrari = Car () ferrari . make_sound ()

以make_sound为例， make_sound是在一个类中定义的，它的第一个参数是self，前面有一个点，所以它是一个方法。

示例 2：函数

def car_make_sound (): print ( "Bloom!" ) car_make_sound ()

这一次car_make_sound()是一个函数，虽然它也有一个 Bloom! 的输出。 但是它上面没有class ，没有任何参数，也没有对象和。 操作员在它之前。

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— Apr 26 4月26日



原文:

HHMathewChan

Posted on Jun 5

Since I am always confused with methods and function, I did some reading about them and here is what I understand.

What confused me

  • methods and functions look the same
  • they both have parenthesis()
  • they can both take argument
  • When defining, they both have def before the name

The difference between methods and functions

  1. Methods cannot call on its own, it need an object to call with, functions can call on it own.
  2. Methods need and dot.operator, functions do not
  3. Methods must be defined within a class, function can define on its own
  4. When defining, methods require at least one parameter, and the first must be self, function can have any number of parameter(including 0)

The follow two program will have an output of "Bloom", but whats the difference?

Example 1: method 

class Car: def make_sound(self): print "Bloom!" ferrari = Car()
ferrari.make_sound()

Take make_sound as example, make_sound is define within a class, it takes self as its first parameter, it has a dot before it, so it is a method.

Example 2: function 

def car_make_sound(): print("Bloom!") car_make_sound()

This time car_make_sound() is a function, although it also has an output of Bloom!. But it does not have class on top, it does not have any parameter and it have not object and . operator before it.

Nitya Narasimhan - May 26

Cristian Fernando - Apr 26
Items.Author: mathewchan

Items.Title: GraphQL 学习路径 | GraphQL learning path
Items.Description:
我的 GraphQL 学习路径的分段故事。

第一次互动

第一个 GraphQL 页面入门链接指向学习页面。 我是第一次学习 GraphQL，感觉很迷茫。 在阅读了学习页面上的主题后，我想知道如何使用 GraphQL。 这个页面想教我什么？ 还有更多的 GraphQL 工具吗？ 我应该使用什么 GraphQL 工具？ 我有很多问题，没有答案。

花费时间：~2 小时。

第二次互动

学习问题分析是这样的：

  • 我不能使用没有服务器的客户端，所以我应该从服务器开始。
  • 他们这样做，很方便。
  • 嗯，GraphQL Apollo 服务器应该是一个好的开始。
  • 我很快就会启动并运行。
  • 在第一页之后，我有一个正在运行的 GraphQL 服务器。

我尝试过的一些事情：

  • 修改查询。
  • 添加一个变量。
  • 阅读有关缓存的信息。
  • 突变。
  • 花费时间：2天

    第 1 天：约 1 小时。

    第 2 天：约 10 小时（包括休息和其他活动）。

    第二轮结论：

    • 对 GraphQL 的多语言支持。
    • 多层主题和相对陡峭的学习曲线。
    • 上手容易，精通难。

    第三次互动

    局势正在升温。 我想使用打字稿，所以我设置了nodemon和ts-node。 五分钟后，打字稿服务器运行。 十五分钟后，我正在查看类型声明。 一个特性蠕动，我想使用模式。 第一个特性蠕变是打字稿。 架构不起作用，我正在阅读阿波罗文档。 四十五分钟后，没有任何效果。 我在浪费时间。 将设置恢复为 javascript 并让服务器再次运行。 我承诺要做一个最小可行的产品。 与功能小兵的斗争仍在继续。

    我正在尝试为 Apollo 创建 GQL 模式，但不起作用。 打开 Apollo 文档，但关于架构的内容不多。 我打开 GQL 文档以了解如何创建模式。 没运气。 等等，这很奇怪。 创建模式的文档不是我第一次使用的文档。 嗯，为什么有多个文档？ 他们有什么区别？ 我打开了旧文档，它使用了 GraphQL express 服务器。 关于同一件事的多个文档。 这些文档是关于同一件事的吗？ 他们对它的描述不同，我想通了。 多层的东西：

    • 解析器。
    • 我们可以在扩展名为 .gql 的文件中编写模式。

    我的天啊...

    我唯一有更多问题的主题是 C++。 昨天我试图弄清楚 C++ 类继承是如何工作的。 为什么父类前面有public这个关键字？ 共有三种类继承模式：私有继承、受保护继承和公共继承。 类继承默认是私有的，但他们需要一个公共的。 我花了半个小时研究才弄明白。

    结束语

    我可以轻松编写入门级 GraphQL 应用程序。 实验之旅很有趣。 当我有心情的时候，我会更多地关注 GraphQL。 这个概念令人兴奋。

    存储库

    在撰写本文时，GraphQL 工作正在进行中。 如果有一个名为 graphql-checkout 的分支，打开它。 在实验目录中是 graphql-checkout 文件夹。

    我的工作在这个存储库中。



原文:

A segmented story of my GraphQL learning path.

The first interaction

The first GraphQL page getting started link leads to the learning page. I am learning GraphQL for the first time and feel lost. After reading the topics on the learning page, I wondered how to use GraphQL. What is this page trying to teach me? What part of the GraphQL is on this page? Are there more GraphQL tools? What GraphQL tool should I use? I had a lot of questions and no answers.

Time spent: ~2 hours.

The second interaction

The learning problem analysis went something like this:

  • GraphQL is a client-server tool.
  • GraphQL client needs a GraphQL server.
  • I can't use a client without a server, so I should start with the server.
  • Does the GraphQL documentation offer a list of tools? They do, handy.
  • Hmm, the GraphQL Apollo server should be a good start.
  • Apollo server documentation looks good. I will be up and running soon.
  • After the first page, I had a running GraphQL server.

Some things I tried:

  • Modify a query.
  • Add a variable.
  • Read about caching.
  • Mutations.
  • ...

Time spent: 2 days

Day 1: ~1 hour.

Day 2: ~10 hours (breaks and other activities included).

Conclusions from the second round:

  • Multi-language support for GraphQL.
  • Multi-layer topic and relatively steep learning curve.
  • Easy to get into but hard to master.

The third interaction

The situation is getting heated. I want to use typescript, so I set up nodemon and ts-node. Five minutes in, and the typescript server runs. Fifteen minutes in, and I'm looking at the type declarations. A feature creep creeps in, and I want to use schema. The first feature creep was the typescript. The schema didn't work, and I'm reading the apollo documentation. Forty-five minutes in, nothing works. I'm wasting time. The javascript server was running, and I didn't need typescript or schema. Restored the setup to javascript and had the server running again. I made a promise to make a minimal viable product. Fighting the feature creeps continues.

I am trying to create a GQL schema for Apollo, doesn't work. Open the Apollo docs, but not much about the schema. TypeDefs and Resolvers are everywhere, but are they the way to go?

I open the GQL documentation to find out how to create a schema. No luck. Wait, that's weird. The docs for creating schema aren't the ones I used the first time. Hmm, why is there more than one documentation? What is their difference? I opened the old documentation, and it used the GraphQL express server. More than one documentation about the same thing. Are these docs about the same thing? They describe it differently, and I figured it out. The multi-layer thing:

  • GraphQL schema.
  • Resolvers.
  • We can write schema in a file with the .gql extension.

Oh my god...

The only topic I have more problems with is C++. Yesterday I tried to figure out how does the C++ class inheritance work. Why is there the keyword public in front of the parent class? There are three class inheritance modes: private, protected, and public inheritance. The class inheritance is by default private, but they needed a public. It took me half an hour of research to figure it out.

Closing words

I can comfortably write entry-level GraphQL apps. The experimental journey was fun. When I'm in the mood, I will look more at GraphQL. The concept feels exciting.

The repository

At the moment of writing, the GraphQL work is in progress. If there is a branch called graphql-checkout, open it. In the experiments directory is the graphql-checkout folder.

My work is in this repository.
